brand.supportingPhrase: Thoughtful landscape planning for properties of every scale.

footer.description: Verdeon helps property decision-makers understand landscape design possibilities, organize project priorities, and identify relevant independent professionals where available.
footer.disclaimer: Verdeon is an independent information and provider-matching platform. Verdeon does not directly perform landscape construction, installation, planting, lighting, or maintenance services. Provider participation and service availability may vary by location.
footer.copyright: © 2026 Verdeon Design Network LLC. All rights reserved.

advertise.title: Advertise & Collaborate
advertise.text: We are always open to new opportunities, high-impact collaborations, and tailored business partnerships. Whether you want to advertise your brand to our audience, launch a joint project, or book our professional services, we are ready to bring your ideas to life. Every business is unique, and we don't believe in one-size-fits-all solutions. Please reach out to us using the contact form below, tell us a bit about your goals, and our team will get back to you with an exclusive, custom-tailored proposal designed strictly for your budget and objectives. Let’s build something great together.

aboutFeedback.title: What Property Owners Value About a Clearer Planning Process.
aboutFeedback.intro: Real feedback can help explain which parts of the landscape planning experience property owners find especially useful.
aboutFeedback.imageAlt: Landscape professional working in a carefully planned garden

items.quote: The planning process helped us organize our outdoor priorities and understand how the main design elements could work together.
items.name: Preview Client

items.quote: A structured project brief made it easier to compare possible directions and prepare more focused questions.
items.name: Preview Client

cookie.text: Verdeon stores a small preference in your browser so the cookie notice does not reappear. No advertising cookies are enabled by this website.

labels.privacyConsent: I have read the Privacy Policy and consent to Verdeon using this information to respond to my request.

placeholders.message: Share the property context, priorities, timing, and any questions you would like us to consider.

form.success: Thank you. Your request was sent to Verdeon and a team member will review it.
form.error: We could not send your request. Your entries are still here—please try again or email support@verdeon.com.
form.validation: Please review the highlighted fields and provide the missing information.

services.title: Residential Landscape Design
services.imageAlt: Private modern backyard with lawn, planting, and seating beside the home
services.secondaryImageAlt: Private garden path with pebbles, hostas, and structured evergreen planting
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Residential backyard seen from above with patio, lawn, hedges, and planting
services.summary: Explore a coordinated plan for circulation, outdoor rooms, planting character, privacy, and long-term project phasing.
services.overview: A residential landscape plan can connect the architecture of a home with the way people move, gather, relax, and view the property. Verdeon helps organize the initial brief and identify relevant planning options.
scope: Property-wide layout and outdoor room relationships
Arrival, circulation, views, and privacy priorities
Planting, hardscape, lighting, and water-use direction
Practical sequencing for single-phase or gradual implementation

deliverables.title: Concept framework
deliverables.text: A high-level organization of zones, connections, and opportunities.

deliverables.title: Planting direction
deliverables.text: Possible character, structure, seasonal interest, and maintenance preferences.

deliverables.title: Visualization options
deliverables.text: Plan-view or three-dimensional studies may help communicate spatial ideas.

faq.q: Can an existing yard be redesigned in stages?
faq.a: Yes. A planning brief may prioritize essential circulation and grading first, followed by planting, lighting, and finishing details as timing and budget allow.

faq.q: Does Verdeon install the landscape?
faq.a: No. Verdeon is an information and provider-matching platform and does not directly perform construction or installation.

faq.q: What information is useful at the start?
faq.a: Property photos, a survey if available, known constraints, inspiration, priorities, and an approximate decision timeline are helpful.

services.title: Commercial Landscape Design
services.imageAlt: Modern commercial courtyard with structured planting between office buildings
services.secondaryImageAlt: Landscape professional installing irrigation around structured commercial planting
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Landscape team caring for formal rose beds and hedges
services.summary: Consider arrival, shared use, accessibility, visibility, and an outdoor identity appropriate to a commercial property.
services.overview: Commercial landscape planning connects site experience with operational realities. A clear brief can help decision-makers compare options for entrances, common areas, planting character, circulation, and future maintenance expectations.
scope: Arrival sequences and public-facing landscape character
Pedestrian circulation and accessibility considerations
Shared outdoor space and tenant or visitor use
Durable planting and materials direction

deliverables.title: Site experience brief
deliverables.text: An organized record of audience, access, visibility, and shared-use priorities.

deliverables.title: Landscape character
deliverables.text: Direction for planting structure, materials, edges, and seasonal presence.

deliverables.title: Coordination notes
deliverables.text: Questions to discuss with qualified designers, engineers, and implementation providers.

faq.q: Can the planning process include multiple stakeholders?
faq.a: Yes. Verdeon can help organize an inquiry that reflects ownership, management, tenant, visitor, and operational priorities.

faq.q: Are accessibility requirements reviewed?
faq.a: Accessibility should be addressed by qualified project professionals. Verdeon can help make it an explicit part of the planning brief.

faq.q: Is provider availability the same in every location?
faq.a: No. Participation, experience, and service availability vary by property type and location.

services.title: Front Yard Design
services.imageAlt: White home with flowering front-yard trees, shrubs, lawn, and entry path
services.secondaryImageAlt: Front yard with curved walkway, layered shrubs, and evergreen structure
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Close-up of a refined lawn edge with white stone mulch and compact shrubs
services.summary: Shape a clear, welcoming arrival with purposeful paths, planting hierarchy, screening, and a strong relationship to the home.
services.overview: A front yard must work as an approach, threshold, view, and neighborhood-facing space. Planning can clarify how access, planting, privacy, drainage, and architectural character should work together.
scope: Entry sequence and path geometry
Driveway, gate, porch, and sidewalk relationships
Screening, openness, and curb-facing planting structure
Rain-garden, slope, or drainage questions to raise with professionals

deliverables.title: Arrival concept
deliverables.text: A clear hierarchy from public edge to front door.

deliverables.title: Planting rhythm
deliverables.text: Potential structural and seasonal layers that support the architecture.

deliverables.title: Priority map
deliverables.text: A practical order for pathways, screening, planting, and lighting decisions.

faq.q: Can a front yard feel private without becoming closed off?
faq.a: Often, layered planting, selective screening, and carefully positioned openings can balance privacy with a welcoming approach.

faq.q: Can driveway and walkway changes be considered together?
faq.a: Yes. Reviewing their geometry together helps clarify safety, hierarchy, and the visual arrival sequence.

faq.q: Are drainage recommendations engineering advice?
faq.a: No. Drainage concerns should be assessed by appropriately qualified professionals. Verdeon helps ensure they are included in the brief.

services.title: Backyard Design
services.imageAlt: Backyard with lawn, curved patio, lounge furniture, clipped trees, and privacy hedges
services.secondaryImageAlt: Evening backyard patio with warm string lights, seating, and layered landscape lighting
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Private garden with stepping-stone path, lawn, walls, and shaded planting
services.summary: Organize dining, gathering, play, privacy, planting, and movement into a backyard that supports everyday life.
services.overview: Backyard planning starts with how the space should feel and function across a typical day. A spatial framework can reduce conflicts between circulation, furniture, planting, utilities, privacy, and changing family needs.
scope: Dining, lounge, play, garden, and flexible-use zones
Connections between the home, patio, lawn, and planting
Privacy, shade, views, and storage considerations
Potential phasing for hardscape, planting, and lighting

deliverables.title: Use-zone plan
deliverables.text: Relationships between dining, relaxing, play, planting, and circulation.

deliverables.title: Comfort direction
deliverables.text: Questions around shade, privacy, exposure, seating, and evening use.

deliverables.title: Implementation sequence
deliverables.text: An order that can help preserve access and avoid reworking completed areas.

faq.q: How much space should be reserved for circulation?
faq.a: That depends on furniture, doors, grade changes, accessibility, and expected use. A scaled plan helps test clearances before decisions are made.

faq.q: Can the design support both entertaining and quiet use?
faq.a: Yes. Separating active and quiet zones while maintaining clear connections can support multiple patterns of use.

faq.q: Can existing trees be part of the brief?
faq.a: Yes. Existing vegetation, shade, roots, condition, and views should be documented for qualified professionals to evaluate.

services.title: Garden & Planting Plans
services.imageAlt: Layered garden with flowering perennials, lawn, shrubs, and a timber path
services.secondaryImageAlt: Hands planting white flowers into fresh garden soil
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Landscape professional selecting flowers inside a bright greenhouse
services.summary: Explore planting structure, seasonal character, spacing, site conditions, and maintenance preferences as one coordinated system.
services.overview: A planting plan can translate desired character into a layered composition that responds to light, soil, exposure, scale, and long-term care expectations. Local suitability must be confirmed by qualified professionals.
scope: Structural trees, shrubs, perennials, and ground layers
Seasonal sequence, texture, color restraint, and winter presence
Light, soil, moisture, exposure, and site limitations
Spacing, establishment, and preferred maintenance intensity

deliverables.title: Planting character
deliverables.text: A concise direction for form, texture, seasonal rhythm, and visual density.

deliverables.title: Layering logic
deliverables.text: Potential relationships between canopy, structure, seasonal layers, and ground plane.

deliverables.title: Suitability questions
deliverables.text: A checklist for confirming local conditions, availability, spacing, and care with professionals.

faq.q: Does a planting plan specify exact plants?
faq.a: It may include a conceptual or detailed palette depending on scope. Final suitability and availability should be confirmed locally.

faq.q: Can lower-maintenance preferences be considered?
faq.a: Yes. Maintenance expectations should be recorded early so density, access, succession, and plant character can be discussed realistically.

faq.q: Can existing planting be retained?
faq.a: Existing plants can be documented for assessment. Retention decisions should reflect health, size, roots, site fit, and the proposed spatial plan.

services.title: Patio & Walkway Design
services.imageAlt: Stepping-stone walkway crossing a private green lawn toward a shaded garden terrace
services.secondaryImageAlt: Curved stone path with planting beds and warm pathway lights at dusk
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Aerial view of a planned backyard with paths, pergola, lawn, and garden zones
services.summary: Consider circulation, scale, grade transitions, materials, edges, and the relationship between paved and planted space.
services.overview: Patios and walkways establish the functional framework of an outdoor space. Early planning can test dimensions, transitions, furniture clearances, drainage questions, and material character before implementation decisions.
scope: Primary and secondary circulation routes
Patio scale, furniture clearances, and door relationships
Steps, ramps, grades, thresholds, and edge conditions
Material direction and relationships with planting

deliverables.title: Circulation diagram
deliverables.text: A hierarchy of primary routes, secondary paths, thresholds, and pauses.

deliverables.title: Material direction
deliverables.text: A coordinated family of surface, edge, step, and transition ideas.

deliverables.title: Technical question list
deliverables.text: Items such as grade, drainage, base preparation, and accessibility for professionals to resolve.

faq.q: Can different paving materials be combined?
faq.a: Yes, but a limited material family and clear hierarchy usually create a more coherent result.

faq.q: Are construction details included?
faq.a: Verdeon does not provide construction documents. Qualified local professionals should resolve structural, drainage, code, and installation details.

faq.q: Can an existing patio be incorporated?
faq.a: It can be documented as part of the planning context so connections, scale, condition, and expansion options can be discussed.

services.title: Outdoor Lighting
services.imageAlt: Backyard patio at night with warm string lights, wall lights, and illuminated planting
services.secondaryImageAlt: Curved garden path with lanterns and low landscape lighting at dusk
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Detailed lawn edge with clipped shrubs, white stone mulch, and clean garden lines
services.summary: Explore a nighttime hierarchy for safe movement, welcoming entrances, focal planting, and comfortable outdoor use.
services.overview: A lighting concept should begin with purpose: safe circulation, useful task light, restrained atmosphere, and selective emphasis. Electrical design and installation must be completed by qualified professionals.
scope: Entry, path, step, and transition priorities
Outdoor dining, gathering, and task-light needs
Selective planting, wall, and architectural emphasis
Glare, color temperature, controls, and dark-sky awareness

deliverables.title: Lighting hierarchy
deliverables.text: A diagram of essential, useful, and atmospheric lighting priorities.

deliverables.title: Mood direction
deliverables.text: Guidance around warmth, contrast, restraint, and focal moments.

deliverables.title: Coordination brief
deliverables.text: Questions for licensed electrical and landscape professionals regarding fixtures, power, controls, and compliance.

faq.q: Does more lighting make a landscape safer?
faq.a: Not necessarily. Good visibility, controlled contrast, clear transitions, and reduced glare can matter more than overall brightness.

faq.q: Does Verdeon perform electrical work?
faq.a: No. Verdeon does not install lighting or perform electrical services.

faq.q: Can lighting be planned before the landscape is installed?
faq.a: Yes. Early coordination can help identify routes, sleeves, power questions, controls, and planting relationships before implementation.

services.title: 3D Landscape Visualization
services.imageAlt: Aerial view of a structured backyard layout with paths, lawn, pergola, and outdoor rooms
services.secondaryImageAlt: Completed backyard composition showing lawn, patio, planting, and circulation from above
services.tertiaryImageAlt: Commercial courtyard composition with clear circulation and planted zones
services.summary: Understand how plan views, perspective studies, and rendered concepts may help communicate scale, materials, planting, and atmosphere.
services.overview: Visualization can make spatial ideas easier to discuss before implementation. The appropriate level of detail depends on the question: a simple massing view, a material study, or a more developed rendered concept.
scope: Plan-view and perspective relationships
Massing, scale, sightline, and spatial studies
Material, planting character, and lighting direction
Viewpoint selection and revision priorities

deliverables.title: Plan-view study
deliverables.text: A top-down framework for layout, scale, circulation, and key relationships.

deliverables.title: Perspective direction
deliverables.text: One or more views focused on important decisions rather than decorative imagery alone.

deliverables.title: Revision record
deliverables.text: Clear notes documenting agreed changes and questions requiring professional confirmation.

faq.q: Is a rendering the same as a construction drawing?
faq.a: No. A visualization communicates spatial or aesthetic direction; it does not replace technical construction documents.

faq.q: How accurate can a 3D study be?
faq.a: Accuracy depends on the quality of survey, dimensions, architecture, product information, and scope supplied to the visualization professional.

faq.q: Do all projects need 3D visualization?
faq.a: No. A clear plan view may be sufficient for many decisions. Visualization is most useful when scale, height, views, or atmosphere are difficult to judge in plan.
